cmo_output.executive_summary: July should establish Innerbloom’s first structured learning cycle rather than chase scale. The available June evidence shows a small Instagram-sourced acquisition signal, some CTA/auth-start events, and product usage concentrated in Innerbloom 2.0 areas, but analytics coverage is partial and previous posts have no measured post-level results. The strategy therefore prioritizes message-market learning around adaptive habits, clearer landing-to-auth intent, and product comprehension using existing assets, with 20 Instagram posts and no final captions.

available_inputs: CMO context for 2026-07 with matching period.current_period
GA4/Search Console analytics sync covering 2026-06-03 to 2026-06-30
Recent campaign snapshot for ib20_mvp with three posts and approval/review metadata
Asset manifest with existing campaign visuals and one Daily Quest product screenshot
Operational constraints for Instagram, square static, square carousel, Metricool CSV, R2/repository assets, and human review
data_gaps: No analytics for 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-02; unavailable dates were not inferred
No post-level performance metrics for prior posts; results array is empty
No published_at or measured_at values for prior posts, so approval does not equal performance
No explicit audience section in strategy memory or structured configuration
No auth_completed count in funnel_events, preventing full registration conversion analysis
No Metricool performance export in the input
Search Console has only 3 impressions and 0 clicks
No qualitative user feedback, interviews, comments, saves, shares, or DM evidence
tracking_issues: accounts.google.com appears as the top raw source and must be treated as auth/referral noise, not acquisition
GA4 active users are cookie/device based and are not registered users
Product page traffic includes repeated sessions from very few active users and cannot be treated as new acquisition
Marketing totals, product totals, and registered users use different definitions
The funnel event list is not a user-level path and cannot prove sequential conversion
Partial coverage warning: requested 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-30, but analytics uses 2026-06-03 to 2026-06-30
assumptions: The July content target is exactly 20 posts because period.target_post_count is 20
Instagram remains the only execution platform because it is the only listed available platform
Square static and square carousel are the only recommended formats because they are the only supported formats
Existing visuals and the Daily Quest screenshot should be reused before requesting new visual generation
The priority audience is inferred from positioning and previous hypotheses: people frustrated by rigid streak-based habit systems; this is an assumption, not validated audience evidence

what_was_attempted: June operated as an MVP acquisition loop with campaign ib20_mvp
The campaign tested adaptive rhythm, anti-perfect-day planning, and a Daily Quest product mechanism angle
Three posts existed in the snapshot: two approved and one needs_review, all for Instagram using carousel/static formats
Tracking URLs included utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content, and ib_post parameters
The operational system proved CSV/R2/Drive/analytics plumbing more than strategic performance
observed_results: Marketing pages recorded 14 active users, 55 sessions, 81 page views, and 256 events
Product pages recorded 45 active users, 370 sessions, 805 page views, and 1273 events, but this is product usage signal, not acquisition
Clean sources show Instagram/social/ib20_mvp with 7 active users, 17 sessions, and 78 page views
Registered users snapshot reports 22 total users, 4 new in period, and 2 excluded internal users
Funnel events include 13 landing_cta_clicked and 12 auth_started events, but no auth_completed count
Search Console recorded 3 impressions and 0 clicks
human_decisions: post_003 remained needs_review in the status field despite an approved_at timestamp in the post metadata
Human review remains required before publishing
operational_issues: No post has measured metrics in the input
Previous campaign results and rejection reasons are empty
Published status is unavailable, so the strategy cannot assume posts generated audience exposure
The admin marketing approval board is still known as seeded/local-storage based until backend persistence exists
Metricool performance data is still manual export
previous_period_analysis.performance_summary: There is enough signal to justify a controlled July learning plan, not enough to declare winning messages. Instagram is the only clean named acquisition source, CTA/auth-start counts exist, and product usage pages show engagement among a small set of users, but partial coverage, missing auth_completed, empty post metrics, and auth referral noise make confidence low.

learnings.learning: The acquisition system can attribute at least some sessions to Instagram campaign traffic, but the sample is small.
evidence: clean_sources reports instagram / social / ib20_mvp with 7 active users, 17 sessions, and 78 screen_page_views
Previous campaign URLs include UTM and ib_post parameters
learnings.implication: Keep Instagram as the July platform and make every post measurable, but avoid scaling to additional channels.

learnings.learning: Landing CTA and auth-start events exist, but the full acquisition-to-registration path is not measurable from the provided funnel.
evidence: registered_users reports 4 newInPeriod but cannot be attributed to posts
learnings.implication: July experiments should optimize observable CTA/auth-start behavior while explicitly treating registration attribution as incomplete.

learnings.learning: Product usage is concentrated in Innerbloom 2.0 pages, especially dashboard, logros, tareas, and task-detail, but this is not proof of new-user activation.
evidence: GA4 active users are not registered users
learnings.implication: Use product mechanism content to explain value, but do not claim that prior marketing caused product engagement.

learnings.learning: Prior creative direction is operationally feasible, but not yet strategically proven.
evidence: Strategy memory says Metricool CSV import, R2 asset ingestion, Instagram publishing, and analytics snapshots work
previous_campaigns results arrays are empty and measured_at values are null
learnings.implication: Reuse the working operational setup while designing sharper hypotheses and decision criteria.

learnings.learning: Search is not currently a meaningful acquisition channel for decisions.
evidence: Search Console shows 3 impressions and 0 clicks
Strategy memory says Search Console is sparse until Google has more impressions
learnings.implication: Do not build July around SEO; keep focus on social message tests.

strategic_diagnosis.primary_problem: Innerbloom has a plausible positioning and basic tracking, but lacks reliable evidence about which message moves strangers from Instagram interest to landing CTA and auth intent.
supporting_problems: Audience definition is inferred rather than validated
The funnel cannot currently confirm auth_completed
Auth/referral noise can distort acquisition interpretation
Product usage evidence is mixed with returning/internal behavior and cannot be treated as acquisition
strategic_diagnosis.why_it_matters: Without a clean first learning cycle, Innerbloom risks producing motivational content volume without knowing which pain, promise, or product mechanism attracts early adopters.
strategic_diagnosis.decision_needed_after_this_period: Decide whether July evidence supports leading with pain/friction, product mechanism/demo, belief differentiation, or early-adopter feedback CTAs for the next monthly cycle.

strategy.primary_objective: Generate measurable early-adopter acquisition learning from Instagram while preserving a clear separation between landing interest, auth intent, registration, activation, and product usage.
secondary_objectives: Improve clarity of Innerbloom’s adaptive-habit positioning through repeated but distinct message tests
Create enough structured evidence for the next CMO run to choose a leading pillar or retire weak angles

priority_audience.description: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks. This audience is inferred from current positioning and prior hypotheses, not directly validated by audience research.
main_pains: Feeling punished when a streak breaks
Restarting from zero after busy or low-energy weeks
Plans that assume every day has the same capacity
Wanting visible progress without shame or perfection pressure

strategy.core_value_proposition: Innerbloom helps people keep direction with adaptive habits that recalibrate to real life instead of forcing perfect consistency.
strategy.period_narrative: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm: build habits that bend without breaking.
strategic_priorities: Validate which entry message creates the clearest acquisition intent: pain/friction, product mechanism, belief differentiation, or early-adopter feedback
Make the landing-to-auth path measurable and interpretable without confusing it with product usage
Reuse existing assets to run a lightweight, reviewable 20-post learning cycle before creating new production complexity

content_pillars.name: Pain and friction
content_pillars.role: Surface the emotional cost of rigid streaks and perfect-day planning
content_pillars.hypothesis: People frustrated by broken streaks and restart cycles will be more likely to visit the landing page and click the CTA
sample_angles: perfect-day planning breaks during real weeks
low-energy weeks should not erase progress
content_pillars.avoid: Do not shame users or claim Innerbloom fixes every consistency problem

content_pillars.name: Product mechanism and demo
content_pillars.role: Make adaptive rhythm concrete through product-backed explanations
content_pillars.hypothesis: Showing Daily Quest, dashboard progress, task intensity, and visible momentum will clarify how the promise works
sample_angles: Daily Quest fits the day available
content_pillars.avoid: Do not imply unlisted features or guarantee outcomes

content_pillars.name: Belief and differentiation
content_pillars.role: Define Innerbloom against rigid productivity and all-or-nothing habit systems
content_pillars.hypothesis: A clear anti-rigidity worldview will attract early adopters who identify with sustainable habit design
content_pillars.avoid: Do not attack specific competitors or overstate comparative proof

content_pillars.name: Early-adopter feedback CTA
content_pillars.role: Invite the right people to try the early product and shape it
content_pillars.hypothesis: Transparent early-stage CTAs will convert curious visitors into auth starts or feedback-ready users
sample_angles: give feedback after using the dashboard
content_pillars.avoid: Do not present the MVP as finished or approved without human review

content_mix.notes: No final captions or post-by-post calendar are included. Head of Content should translate this mix into drafts after human review of the strategy.

experiments.name: Pain-first hooks versus product-first hooks
experiments.hypothesis: Pain/friction posts will produce a higher landing CTA rate than product mechanism posts because the audience problem is easier to recognize than the product solution.
experiments.metric: landing_cta_clicked divided by landing page sessions for posts in each pillar, using UTM and ib_post where available
experiments.decision_criterion: If one pillar produces at least 2x the CTA rate of the other with at least 10 landing sessions per tested pillar, prioritize that pillar next month; otherwise classify as inconclusive.

experiments.name: Carousel explanation versus static statement
experiments.hypothesis: Carousels will improve scroll/engagement and CTA intent for explanatory concepts compared with static statements.
experiments.metric: scroll events and landing_cta_clicked per session by format for UTM-tagged posts
experiments.decision_criterion: If carousel posts exceed static posts by 30% or more on CTA per session with comparable sessions, keep carousel for explanatory topics; otherwise reserve carousel for product demos only.

experiments.name: Early-stage transparency CTA
experiments.hypothesis: Posts that explicitly say the product is early and feedback is welcome will generate more auth_started events per landing session than generic try-it CTAs.
experiments.metric: auth_started per landing session for Early-adopter feedback CTA posts
experiments.decision_criterion: If feedback CTA posts generate at least 3 auth_started events and outperform non-feedback posts on auth_started per session, keep transparent early-adopter CTAs; otherwise rewrite the CTA proposition.

experiments.name: Product screenshot comprehension test
experiments.hypothesis: Using the available Daily Quest screenshot will make the adaptive habit mechanism more concrete and increase landing CTA clicks versus abstract product mechanism visuals.
experiments.metric: landing_cta_clicked per session and dashboard_view event count for product mechanism posts that use screenshots versus reusable abstract visuals
experiments.decision_criterion: If screenshot-backed posts show higher CTA per session and at least one downstream dashboard_view event, request more product screenshots next cycle; otherwise reuse screenshots sparingly.

tone: Concrete enough for a product demo but not overpromising
ctas_allowed: Start with a rhythm that fits this week
Give feedback after trying the dashboard
messages_to_avoid: Final captions, full scripts, or post-by-post calendar in this CMO output
Claims that Innerbloom guarantees habit success
Claims that June content caused registrations or product usage
Equating GA4 active users with registered users
Treating dashboard views as new-user activation without user-level evidence
Medical, therapeutic, or mental-health treatment claims
Competitor attacks or unsupported superiority claims
messaging_guidelines.required_tracking_language: Every drafted post must preserve utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content, and ib_post parameters.

principles: Prefer existing Innerbloom 2.0 screenshots and proven campaign visuals over generic lifestyle imagery
Keep square static and carousel slides readable on mobile
Use dark/light product assets when available, but do not invent unavailable assets
Use R2 or repository-public URLs where execution requires public assets
asset_reuse_rules: Reuse post_001 carousel visual language for pain/friction when appropriate
Reuse post_002 static pain visual language for direct anti-perfect-day statements
Use the Daily Quest screenshot only for product mechanism/demo concepts that actually reference task selection or daily adaptation
Generate new visuals only if the existing asset library cannot support the approved Head of Content draft
missing_assets_to_note: No broad brand asset library is listed
Only one product screenshot is available in the input
No reusable template inventory is listed

do: Create 20 Instagram draft posts from the pillar mix after this draft strategy is reviewed
Separate acquisition, registration, activation, and product usage intent in post hypotheses
Attach a measurable hypothesis and target metric to each draft
Use approved CTAs and full UTM/ib_post tracking
Flag any draft that requires a new asset before producing it
do_not: Do not publish or schedule content from this CMO strategy alone
Do not approve this strategy automatically
Do not write claims based on unmeasured June performance
Do not create captions that imply final product maturity
Do not add unsupported product features
approval_criteria: Draft maps to one of the four approved pillars
Draft has one primary funnel intent
Draft includes a measurable metric and tracking plan
Draft is consistent with adaptive habits, real-life rhythm, recalibration, and progress without perfection pressure
rejection_criteria: Claim relies on missing metrics or invented results
Post confuses product usage with acquisition
Post needs unavailable assets without flagging the gap
Post exceeds the approved 20-post plan or creates a post-by-post calendar in this strategy stage

primary_metrics: Landing page sessions/page views from UTM-tagged Instagram posts
auth_started events by campaign where attribution is available
secondary_metrics: dashboard_view events as activation proxy only when interpreted separately
registered_users.newInPeriod as period context, not post attribution unless linked

funnel_separation.acquisition: landing page views/sessions and clean Instagram campaign source
funnel_separation.registration_completion: auth_completed is a required gap because it is missing from the input
funnel_separation.product_usage: /innerbloom2 and dashboard-v3 pages; not equivalent to acquisition

reporting_requirements: Exclude accounts.google.com referral noise from acquisition decisions
Report partial coverage if any dates are missing
Report results by pillar and format, not only aggregate totals
Mark experiments inconclusive when thresholds are not met

what_to_preserve: Current positioning around adaptive habits, real-life rhythm, recalibration instead of restart, and progress without perfection pressure
Use of UTM plus ib_post tracking
what_changes_for_july: Shift from technical proof to structured message-learning cycle
Limit July to four pillars and four measurable experiments
Treat previous June content as operational proof, not performance proof
open_questions_for_next_period: Which pillar produces the strongest CTA rate?
Can auth_completed be added to the funnel report?
Do feedback CTAs produce auth starts from the right audience?
Do product screenshots improve comprehension enough to justify creating more assets?
Can post-level Metricool data be joined with GA4/UTM results?

brand.objective: Acquire early adopter users for Innerbloom 2.0 with lightweight, measurable social content. The system should minimize manual marketing operations while keeping final approval under human control.
positioning: Innerbloom helps people build sustainable habits that adapt to real life instead of forcing rigid streaks or perfect-day productivity.
content_rules: GA4 active users are anonymous, cookie/device-based users. They are not the same as registered users in Neon.
For marketing decisions:
Treat landing page views as acquisition signal.
Treat `/innerbloom2/...` pages as product usage signal.
Exclude auth redirects such as `accounts.google.com`.
Exclude internal/admin emails before reading registered-user growth.
Search Console is sparse until Google has more impressions.
This is the first real monthly plan once draft generation is wired.
Planned mix:
8 pain/friction posts: streak pressure, perfect-day planning, restarting from zero, habits collapsing during messy weeks.
5 product mechanism/demo posts: adaptive rhythm, weekly recalibration, dashboard progress, task intensity, visible momentum.
4 belief/differentiation posts: real weeks over perfect calendars, progress without shame, sustainable habit design, anti-all-or-nothing routines.
3 early-adopter CTA posts: try the early version, help shape the product, give feedback after using the dashboard.
Creative rules:
Prefer real Innerbloom 2.0 screenshots over generic lifestyle imagery.
Use both dark-mode and light-mode product assets when available.
Keep carousel slides readable on mobile.
Use R2 URLs in Metricool CSV output.
Store source assets and planning context in Google Drive for browsing.
Measurement:
Primary acquisition: landing views and CTA events from UTM-tagged links.
Primary activation: `auth_started -> auth_completed -> dashboard_view`.
Product-interest proxy: views of `/innerbloom2/dashboard`, `/innerbloom2/tareas`, `/innerbloom2/task-detail`, and `/innerbloom2/logros`.
For the next monthly run:
Keep the number of configurable knobs small.
Prefer strong hooks and measurable hypotheses over generic motivational posts.
Reuse visual patterns that remain readable on mobile.
Generate only new visuals when the existing asset library cannot support the post.
Preserve every decision and result in this strategy memory.

known_product_changes: Metricool can ingest image URLs from Cloudflare R2 URLs.
Instagram posts published successfully from the generated CSV.
Cloudflare R2 is now the publishing asset store for CSV exports.
Google Drive remains the human-readable marketing library.
GA4 and Search Console snapshots can be synced into Neon and shown in `/admin/marketing`.

approved_claim_source.required_tracking_language: Every drafted post must preserve utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content, and ib_post parameters.

existing_visuals.label: Your habits should adapt to your real life.
Most habit apps assume every day is the same.
Lower the intensity. Keep the direction.
Build a Journey that can survive real weeks.
If your plan only works on perfect days, it is not a plan.

operational_constraints.publishing_method: Approved posts are exported to Metricool CSV; this exporter does not publish content.
operational_constraints.public_asset_store: Public marketing files can be served from R2 or repository public assets; this exporter does not upload binaries.
calendar_rules: Use dates inside the publishing window
Do not publish or approve posts
